description: The pair potential \( V(r) \) describes the effective interaction energy between two particles as a function of their separation \( r \). The functional form combines a steep, short-range repulsive term, given by \( r^{-15} \), with a longer-range oscillatory contribution, expressed as \( r^{-3} \cos(k(r - 1.25) - \phi) \). Here, \( k \) denotes the oscillation frequency, which controls the period of the potential wells, and \( \phi \) represents a phase shift that determines the positions of the minima and maxima relative to the origin. The constant offset of 1.25 within the cosine argument shifts the oscillation pattern. The \( r^{-3} \) prefactor ensures the oscillation amplitude decays algebraically with distance. This structure models a repulsive core at very short separations, followed by a series of damped attractive wells, mimicking the Friedel oscillations characteristic of metallic systems.
